title: Getting Started on Your Project
author: Bonnie L Hurwitz
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.jqrcmv6

steps.title: Login to the HPC and move...
steps.instruction: Login to the HPC and move over to the ocelote cluster. ssh hpcocelote ssh hpc ocelote

steps.title: Create a class directory
steps.instruction: Create a class directory. Choose a user name for storing your project files. All files for the class project will be written to this directory. change "username" to YOUR user name. mkdir /rsgrps/bh_class/username

steps.title: Go into your user directory
steps.instruction: Go into your user directory. Change "username" to YOUR user name. cd /rsgrps/bh_class/username

steps.title: Create directories for downloading fastq project files
steps.instruction: Create directories for downloading fastq project files. We will download the paired reads from the SRA. mkdir fastqcd fastq mkdir fastq cd fastq

steps.title: Create a file called "list" with...
steps.instruction: Create a file called "list" with all of your SRR file names. nano list

steps.title: using nano edit the get-fastq
steps.instruction: using nano edit the get-fastq.sh script by adding the commands. This script runs on a node on the HPC (we will execute in a step below). The script uses the sra-tookit to download files from Genbank (a public sequence repository). It downloads the files as paired ends if applicable. #!/bin/bash#PBS -W group_list=bh_class#PBS -q standard#PBS -l select=9:ncpus=28:mem=27gb#PBS -l pvmem=235gb#PBS -l walltime=48:00:00#PBS -l cput=48:00:00#PBS -M username@email.arizona.edu#PBS -m beamodule load sratoolsecho “my job_id is: ${PBS_JOBID}”FASTQ_DIR= #!/bin/bash #PBS -W group_list=bh_class #PBS -q standard #PBS -l select=9:ncpus=28:mem=27gb #PBS -l pvmem=235gb #PBS -l walltime=48:00:00 #PBS -l cput=48:00:00 #PBS -M username@email.arizona.edu #PBS -m bea module load sratools echo “my job_id is: ${PBS_JOBID}” FASTQ_DIR="/rsgrps/bh_class/username/fastq" export $FASTQ_DIR cd $FASTQ_DIR for file in `cat list`; do fastq-dump --outdir $FASTQ_DIR --gzip --skip-technical --readids --dumpbase --split-files --clip $file; done

steps.title: Create a script for downloading the SRR files touch get-fastq
steps.instruction: Create a script for downloading the SRR files touch get-fastq.shchmod 755 get-fastq.sh touch get-fastq.sh chmod 755 get-fastq.sh

steps.title: mkdir std-err std-outqsub -o std-out -e std-err get-fastq
steps.instruction: mkdir std-err std-outqsub -o std-out -e std-err get-fastq.sh mkdir std-err std-out qsub -o std-out -e std-err get-fastq.sh you will see where the job runs: $ qsub -o std-out -e std-err get-fastq.sh815336.head1.cm.cluster

steps.title: You can check if you job...
steps.instruction: You can check if you job is running using the qstat command. qstat $ qstat | egrep hur815336.head1 get-fastq.sh bhurwitz 0 Q oc_standard
